name: AARRR: Pirate Metrics
summary: A startup growth framework that tracks five key stages: Acquisition, Activation, Retention, Revenue, and Referral.
whenToUseIt: Use AARRR when building or optimising a growth engine, especially for digital products and startups. It helps identify where your biggest drop-off or opportunity is.
steps: Acquisition: How do users find you? (SEO, ads, social, referrals) — measure: new visitors, signups
Activation: Do they have a great first experience? — measure: onboarding completion, first key action
Retention: Do they come back? — measure: return visits, repeat purchases, DAU/MAU ratio
Revenue: Do they pay you? — measure: conversion to paid, ARPU, CLV
Referral: Do they tell others? — measure: referral rate, NPS, viral coefficient
example: Dropbox famously optimised each stage: Acquisition (referral program giving free storage), Activation (simple onboarding — just drop a file), Retention (files sync automatically so you use it daily), Revenue (storage limits push to paid plans), Referral (invite friends = more free space). Their referral program alone grew signups by 60%.
